Gao Jie
Google Scholar CV

My first name, “Jie”(洁), pronounced like “Jyeah” in English.

I am currently a postdoctoral researcher in Mens, Manus, and Machina (M3S) team at the Singapore-MIT Alliance for Research and Technology, working with Prof. Thomas W. Malone. Prior to this, I did my PhD in Information Systems Technology and Design at Singapore University of Technology and Design, advised by Simon Perrault. My research interests include Human-AI Collaboration, Human-AI Interaction and AI for Social Science. Specifically, I am passionate about:

  • Human-LLM Collaboration/Interaction: investigating the various modes of collaboration/interaction between humans and LLMs, building taxonomy between human-LLM collaboration/interaction activities. One of the main use cases I will explore is code auditing;
  • AI4SocialScience: exploring efficient AI-assisted tools to enhance qualitative analysis, with a special focus on collaboration.

📧 Email: gaojie056[AT]gmail.com

News

2024.01 🎉 One first-authored paper "CollabCoder" and one coauthored paper "Help Me Reflect" have been accepted by CHI2024! (Preprints will have minor refinements in final versions.)

2024.01 🚀 I successfully defended my PhD thesis titled "Tool Support for Human-AI Collaboration in Qualitative Data Analysis".

2023.12 🚀 We will organize a workshop "LLMs as Research Tools" at CHI2024, from 11-16 May 2024 in Honolulu, Hawaii, USA.

2023.12 🚀 Starting from early 2024, I am excited to become a part of the Singapore-MIT Alliance for Research and Technology as a Postdoctoral Researcher, where I will be mentored by Prof. Thomas W. Malone. I'm thrilled about this opportunity!

2023.12 🚀 I attended EMNLP2023 at Singapore. Happy to learn many new things and friends!

2023.11 🚀 Presented CollabCoder project in the Immersive Reality Lab at NUS. Thanks for the invitation from Prof Khoo Eng Tat!

2023.10 🚀 Attended CSCW2023 and presented CollabCoder demo virtually! (due to visa and funding issues)

2023.08 🎉 One coauthored paper "GlassMessaging" has been accepted by Ubicomp2023!

2023.06 🎉 One coauthored paper has been accepted by ICSME2023!

2023.06 🎉 One coauthored paper "VISAR" has been accepted by UIST2023!

2023.06 🎉 One first-authored paper "CoAIcoder" has been accepted by ToCHI!

2023.06 🎉 Our "CollabCoder" platform has been selected for a demonstration at CSCW2023!

2023.01 🚀 Excited to start as a visiting Ph.D. student at Notre Dame, working with Prof Toby's team!

2023.01 Work as an associate chair for the Late-Breaking Work Committee of CHI2023.

2022.06 🚀 Thrilled to begin as a visiting Ph.D. student at NUS-HCI lab, looking forward to collaborating with Prof Shen and the lab team!

2022.04 - 2022.5 Glad to attend CHI2022 in person in New Orleans, LA, USA. Thanks for the funding support from Simon.

2022.01 - 2022.2 Worked as an associate chair for the Late-Breaking Work Committee of CHI2022.

Publications

CollabCoder: A Lower-barrier, Rigorous Workflow for Inductive Collaborative Qualitative Analysis with Large Language Models

Jie Gao, Yuchen Guo, Gionnieve Lim, Tianqin Zhang, Zheng Zhang, Toby Jia-Jun Li, Simon Tangi Perrault.

CHI 2024 LLM+Social Science PDF

Help Me Reflect: Leveraging Self-Reflection Interface Nudges to Enhance Deliberativeness on Online Deliberation Platforms

Shun Yi Yeo, Gionnieve Lim, Jie Gao, Weiyu Zhang, Simon Tangi Perrault

CHI 2024 PDF

CoAIcoder: Examining the Effectiveness of AI-assisted Human-to-Human Collaboration in Qualitative Analysis.

Jie Gao, Kenny Tsu Wei Choo, Junming Cao, Roy Ka Wei Lee, Simon Perrault.

TOCHI 2023 AI4SocialScience PDF

CollabCoder: A GPT-Powered Workflow for Collaborative Qualitative Analysis.

Jie Gao, Yuchen Guo, Toby Jia-Jun Li, Simon Tangi Perrault.

CSCW demo 2023 LLM+Social Science PDF

VISAR: A Human-AI Argumentative Writing Assistant with Visual Programming and Rapid Draft Prototyping.

Zheng Zhang, Jie Gao, Ranjodh Singh Dhaliwal, Toby Jia-Jun Li.

UIST 2023 LLM+Writing PDF

GlassMessaging: Towards Ubiquitous Messaging Using OHMDs.

Nuwan Janaka, Jie Gao, Lin Zhu, Shengdong Zhao, Lan Lyu, Peisen Xu, Maximilian Nabokow, Silang Wang, Yanch Ong

IMWUT 2023 SmartGlass PDF

Characterizing the Complexity and Its Impact on Testing in ML-Enabled Systems: A Case Sutdy on Rasa.

Junming Cao, Bihuan Chen, Longjie Hu, Jie Gao, Kaifeng Huang, Xin Peng.

Differences of Challenges of Working from Home (WFH) between Weibo and Twitter Users during COVID-19.
